name: Razorpay Partner API — Pact Contract Testing
scenario: Razorpay is refactoring its payment API. The team needs to ensure none of the 3 key partner integrations (Flipkart, Zomato, HDFC Bank webhook) break. Implement consumer-driven contract tests so any breaking change is caught in CI before deployment.

core.description: PactStates class: static final String constants for all provider states used in tests. At least 3 constants (one per consumer).
FlipkartCheckoutConsumerTest: @Pact for POST /api/payments → 201 {paymentId:String, status:String, amountPaise:Number}. @Test verifies paymentId and status present.
ZomatoReconciliationConsumerTest: @Pact for GET /api/payments/{id} → 200 {paymentId:String, gateway:String, amountPaise:Number}. @Test verifies all 3 fields present.
PaymentErrorConsumerTest: @Pact for GET missing payment → 404 {error:String(exact='PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND'), message:String}. @Test verifies 404 status and error code.
All 4 @Test methods pass. Each consumer class uses @ExtendWith(PactConsumerTestExt.class) + unique port. MockServer used in each @Test.
README: explains can-i-deploy and why consumer-driven contracts prevent breaking changes.

bonus.description: HdfcWebhookConsumerTest: @Pact for POST /api/webhooks → 200 {received:String}. Test: webhook acknowledged correctly.
MessagePact for Kafka: InventoryService expects PaymentCompletedEvent {paymentId, merchantId, amountPaise}. Uses @MessagePact and MessagePactBuilder.
Provider verification stub: @Provider('razorpay-payment-api') + @PactFolder + @State for each consumer state. @TestTemplate + PactVerificationInvocationContextProvider.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Each @PactTestFor needs a unique port number: Pact starts a mock server on that port — if two test classes share a port, the second fails to bind. @PactTestFor(pactMethod='xxx'): the string must EXACTLY match the method name of the @Pact-annotated method in the same class. stringType vs stringValue: use stringType for IDs and dynamic values (any String accepted), stringValue for error codes that must be exact strings (PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND). PactStates constants: the @State string on the provider side must EXACTLY match the .given() string on the consumer side — use shared constants. .toPact(): always the terminal method — missing it causes compile error.
